title: Language Server + HTTP API
problem: Editors need real-time feature provenance overlaid on code (hover cards, code lens, diagnostics) without polling the filesystem on every keystroke. CI pipelines and browser tools need a stable HTTP API to query the same index.
analysis: A single long-running process serves both transports. The LSP stdio transport connects to any LSP-capable editor. The HTTP server on :7474 provides a REST API for CI, browser extensions, and the webapp. A shared in-memory FeatureIndex (chokidar-backed) is the single source of truth for both transports. As of iteration 3, 10 Vitest tests cover HTTP routing, rate limiting, lineage, and oversized-body protection.
implementation: LacLspServer orchestrates FeatureIndex + optional HttpServer + optional LSP connection. LSP capabilities: hover (with missing fields + completeness %), codeLens, definition, diagnostics (push), workspaceSymbols. HTTP endpoints: GET /health (uptime, indexedCount, version) /features /features/:key /features/:key/lineage /blame /lint /events (SSE), PUT /features/:key, DELETE /features/:key. Startup log: 'lac-lsp v0.1.0 started on port N'. File watching via chokidar.
validation: 10 Vitest tests: GET /health, GET /features, GET /features?q= filter, GET /blame path-traversal guard, PUT valid update, PUT oversized body (413), DELETE missing key, rate limiter structure, SSE connection.

decisions.decision: Single process for both LSP and HTTP
decisions.rationale: One shared FeatureIndex means no sync needed between transports. Simpler to install and operate.
alternativesConsidered: separate processes — double the memory, sync complexity

decisions.rationale: fs.watch --recursive is unreliable on Linux/Docker. Chokidar provides consistent cross-platform behavior.

knownLimitations: Only one workspace root supported — multi-root workspaces use first folder only
